title: LichenFrame.from_bytes allows zero-length payload
description:
File: python/src/lichen/link/frame.py:183-188

The frame parsing allows zero-length payload:

    if length < offset + addr_len + mic_len:
        raise FrameError('frame too short for declared address/MIC sizes')
    
    dst_addr = body[offset : offset + addr_len]
    offset += addr_len
    payload = body[offset : len(body) - mic_len]

If length == offset + addr_len + mic_len exactly, payload will be empty bytes.

While the spec may allow this, link_layer.py:431-436 requires payload to contain at least SIGNATURE_LENGTH (48) bytes when signature_present is True:

    if len(frame.payload) < SIGNATURE_LENGTH:
        logger.warning(...)
        return None

This is handled at runtime, but the frame parser should validate based on signature_present flag.

Fix: Add validation in from_bytes() that payload length is sufficient for signature if signature_present bit is set.
close_reason: Fixed: Added validation in from_bytes() to ensure payload >= 48 bytes when signature_present flag is set. Updated tests to use valid payloads and added explicit test for the new validation.
